training_and_evaluation.modules¶

training_and_evaluation.modules.evaluator¶

Comprehensive model evaluator for test set evaluation.

This module provides three evaluator classes for assessing time-series classification models on test data:

  • UEAEvaluator – lightweight evaluator for UEA benchmark datasets, accumulating per-dataset metrics and saving a JSON report.

  • TruckEvaluator – full evaluator for the truck dataset, computing a rich set of classification metrics and producing publication-quality confusion matrix and per-class metric plots.

  • TruckEvaluatorBenchmark – subclass of TruckEvaluator for evaluating sklearn-compatible benchmark models (e.g. predict() API) directly on pre-loaded arrays rather than a DataLoader.

class training_and_evaluation.modules.evaluator.TruckEvaluator(cfg: DictConfig, model: Module, test_loader: DataLoader, save_dir: Path)[source]¶

Bases: object

Full evaluator for the truck time-series classification dataset.

Loads the best saved model checkpoint, runs inference over a DataLoader, computes a comprehensive set of classification metrics (weighted, macro, micro, per-class), and saves a JSON results file together with publication-quality confusion matrix and per-class metric plots.

cfg¶

Full Hydra/OmegaConf configuration object. Expected keys include training.device, training.num_classes, and training.label.

Type:

DictConfig

model¶

Model to evaluate, moved to device.

Type:

nn.Module

test_loader¶

DataLoader for the test set.

Type:

DataLoader

save_results_dir_path¶

Directory where results and plots are saved.

Type:

Path

Example

>>> evaluator = TruckEvaluator(cfg, model, test_loader, save_dir=Path("results/"))
>>> evaluator.load_best_model()
>>> results = evaluator.evaluate()
>>> print(results["overall_metrics"]["f1_weighted"])
compute_and_save_metrics(preds: ndarray, targets: ndarray) → dict[source]¶

Computes a full suite of classification metrics and saves them to JSON.

Computes weighted, macro, and micro averages for precision, recall, and F1, along with per-class breakdowns, Matthews correlation coefficient, Cohen’s kappa, and a confusion matrix. Saves everything to test_results.json inside save_results_dir_path.

Parameters:

  • preds (np.ndarray) – Predicted class indices of shape (num_samples,).

  • targets (np.ndarray) – Ground-truth class indices of shape (num_samples,).

Returns:

Nested results dictionary with keys "overall_metrics",

"per_class_metrics", "confusion_matrix", "num_samples", and "num_classes".

Return type:

Dict

evaluate() → dict[source]¶

Runs full test set evaluation and saves metrics and plots.

Iterates over test_loader, collects predictions and targets, then calls _compute_and_save_metrics(), _plot_confusion_matrix(), and _plot_per_class_metrics().

Returns:

Results dictionary as returned by

_compute_and_save_metrics(), containing "overall_metrics", "per_class_metrics", "confusion_matrix", "num_samples", and "num_classes".

Return type:

Dict

get_class_names() → list[str][source]¶

Resolves ordered human-readable class names from the label decoder.

Handles label remapping for "highway_label" and "weather_label" columns via their respective new-to-old index maps, and falls back to direct decoder lookup for all other labels.

Returns:

Ordered list of class name strings, one per class index.

Return type:

List[str]

load_best_model() → None[source]¶

Loads the best model checkpoint from disk into model.

Looks for best_model.pt inside save_results_dir_path. Supports checkpoints saved as a full state-dict or as a dict with a "model_state_dict" key. Logs the validation F1 and epoch number if present in the checkpoint.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If the checkpoint file does not contain a valid model state dict.

plot_confusion_matrix(cm: list[list[int]]) → None[source]¶

Renders and saves a styled confusion matrix heatmap.

Produces a square heatmap with a gold gradient colormap, black grid lines, and cell-level count annotations. Saves the figure as both confusion_matrix.pdf and confusion_matrix.png inside save_results_dir_path.

Parameters:

cm (List[List[int]]) – Confusion matrix as a nested list of integer counts, as returned by confusion_matrix(...).tolist().

plot_per_class_metrics(results: dict) → None[source]¶

Renders and saves a 2x2 grid of per-class metric bar charts.

Produces bar charts for per-class accuracy, precision, recall, and F1, each annotated with the metric value and sample count. Bars exceeding the macro average are highlighted in dark grey; others in light grey. A dashed gold line marks the macro average. Saves the figure as both per_class_metrics.pdf and per_class_metrics.png inside save_results_dir_path.

Parameters:

results (Dict) – Results dictionary as returned by _compute_and_save_metrics().

class training_and_evaluation.modules.evaluator.TruckEvaluatorBenchmark(cfg: DictConfig, model, test_data, targets, save_dir: Path)[source]¶

Bases: TruckEvaluator

Evaluator for sklearn-compatible benchmark models on the truck dataset.

Extends TruckEvaluator by overriding __init__() and evaluate() to accept pre-loaded arrays and a predict()-style model interface instead of a PyTorch DataLoader.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Expected keys include training.num_classes and training.label.

Type:

DictConfig

model¶

Sklearn-compatible model exposing a predict(X) method.

test_data¶

Input features in whatever format model.predict() expects (e.g. a numpy array or pandas DataFrame).

targets¶

Ground-truth class indices, convertible to np.ndarray of shape (num_samples,).

save_dir¶

Directory where JSON results and plots are saved.

Type:

Path

Example

>>> evaluator = TruckEvaluatorBenchmark(
...     cfg=cfg,
...     model=sklearn_model,
...     test_data=X_test,
...     targets=y_test,
...     save_dir=Path("results/benchmark/"),
... )
>>> results = evaluator.evaluate()
>>> print(results["overall_metrics"]["f1_weighted"])
evaluate() → dict[source]¶

Runs evaluation using the benchmark model’s predict() interface.

Calls model.predict(test_data) directly, then delegates to _compute_and_save_metrics(), _plot_confusion_matrix(), and _plot_per_class_metrics() inherited from TruckEvaluator.

Returns:

Results dictionary as returned by

_compute_and_save_metrics(), containing "overall_metrics", "per_class_metrics", "confusion_matrix", "num_samples", and "num_classes".

Return type:

Dict

class training_and_evaluation.modules.evaluator.UEAEvaluator(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Lightweight evaluator for UEA benchmark time-series classification datasets.

Iterates over multiple datasets, computes accuracy, balanced accuracy, and weighted F1 for each, accumulates the results, and can save a consolidated JSON report with per-dataset and average metrics.

cfg¶

Full Hydra/OmegaConf configuration object. Expected keys include model.name, training.device, and training.save_results_dir_path.

Type:

DictConfig

Example

>>> evaluator = UEAEvaluator(cfg)
>>> for dataset_name, (model, loader) in datasets.items():
...     evaluator.evaluate_model(model, loader, dataset_name)
>>>
>>> evaluator.save_evaluation_report()
compute_average_metrics() → dict[str, float][source]¶

Computes mean and standard deviation of metrics across all evaluated datasets.

Aggregates the per-dataset results stored in results and returns a summary dictionary. Must be called after at least one evaluate_model() call.

Returns:

Aggregated metrics with keys "avg_accuracy",

"avg_f1_score", "avg_b_accuracy", "std_accuracy", "std_f1_score", "std_b_accuracy", and "num_datasets".

Return type:

Dict[str, float]

evaluate_model(model: Module, test_loader: DataLoader, dataset_name: str) → dict[str, float][source]¶

Evaluates a model on a single UEA dataset.

Runs inference over the full test set, computes accuracy, balanced accuracy, and weighted F1, stores the result under dataset_name, and logs a summary line.

Parameters:

  • model (nn.Module) – Trained PyTorch model to evaluate.

  • test_loader (DataLoader) – DataLoader yielding (data, targets) batches for the test set.

  • dataset_name (str) – Identifier for the dataset, used as the key in results.

Returns:

Metrics for this dataset with keys

"accuracy", "b_accuracy", "f1_score", and "num_samples".

Return type:

Dict[str, float]

save_evaluation_report() → Path[source]¶

Saves a consolidated JSON evaluation report to disk.

Calls compute_average_metrics(), then writes a JSON file containing the model name, average metrics, per-dataset results, and dataset count to save_results_dir_path / "evaluation_report.json".

Returns:

Absolute path to the saved JSON report file.

Return type:

Path

training_and_evaluation.modules.trainer¶

Training module for neural network model training and evaluation.

This module provides a comprehensive Trainer class for training PyTorch neural network models.

Example

>>> model = torch.nn.Linear(10, 2)
>>> trainer = Trainer(cfg, model, train_loader, val_loader, save_dir)
>>> trained_model, total_time = trainer.fit()
class training_and_evaluation.modules.trainer.Trainer(cfg: DictConfig, model: Module, train_loader: DataLoader, val_loader: DataLoader | None, save_dir: Path)[source]¶

Bases: object

A trainer class for training and evaluating PyTorch neural network models.

This class handles the complete training pipeline including optimizer setup, learning rate scheduling, validation, checkpointing, and visualization of training metrics.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Expected keys are device, learning_rate, weight_decay, betas, epochs, label_smoothing, max_grad_norm, warmup_ratio, alpha_f, restart_interval, patience.

Type:

DictConfig

model¶

The neural network model to train.

Type:

nn.Module

train_loader¶

DataLoader for training data.

Type:

DataLoader

val_loader¶

DataLoader for validation data.

Type:

DataLoader | None

save_results_dir_path¶

Directory path for saving results and checkpoints.

Type:

Path

fit() → tuple[Module, float][source]¶

Execute the complete training loop with validation and early stopping.

Trains the model for the specified number of epochs, validating after each epoch. Implements early stopping based on F1-score improvement. Saves the best model and generates training visualizations at the end.

Returns:

A tuple containing:

  • Trained model (nn.Module): The best model loaded from checkpoint

  • Total training time (float): Total training time in minutes

Return type:

tuple[nn.Module, float]

log_training_config() → None[source]¶

Log training configuration at the start of training.

Outputs device, learning rate, and number of epochs to the logger.

Returns:

None

plot_training_curves() → None[source]¶

Generate and save training visualization plots.

Creates two figures: 1. Loss and Learning Rate (2x1 subplots) with training/validation loss and LR 2. Validation Metrics (2x2 subplots) with accuracy, F1-score, precision, recall

Plots are saved as both PNG (300 dpi) and PDF formats. Includes custom font loading and professional styling.

Returns:

None

save_checkpoint(epoch: int, is_best: bool = False) → None[source]¶

Save model checkpoint to disk.

Saves the best model state dictionary (for inference) when is_best=True. The checkpoint includes the model state dict and metadata about the epoch and validation metrics.

Parameters:

  • epoch (int) – Current epoch number for metadata.

  • is_best (bool, optional) – Whether this is the best model so far. If True, saves to ‘best_model.pt’. Defaults to False.

Returns:

None

setup_cosine_scheduler(alpha_f: float = 0.1) → LambdaLR[source]¶

Setup cosine annealing scheduler with linear warmup.

Implements a learning rate schedule that: 1. Linearly increases LR from 0 to initial_lr during warmup 2. Decays LR using cosine annealing to alpha_f * initial_lr after warmup

Parameters:

alpha_f (float, optional) – Minimum learning rate as a fraction of initial LR. Defaults to 0.1. Can be overridden by cfg.training.alpha_f.

Returns:

Learning rate scheduler object.

Return type:

torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR

setup_cosine_scheduler_multi_restart() → LambdaLR[source]¶

Setup cosine annealing scheduler with multiple warm restarts.

Implements a learning rate schedule with periodic restarts: - The schedule repeats itself every restart_interval epochs - Each cycle includes warmup followed by cosine decay - This allows the optimizer to escape local minima multiple times

Returns:

Learning rate scheduler with restarts.

Return type:

torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR

setup_cosine_scheduler_restart() → CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts[source]¶

Setup cosine annealing with warm restarts using PyTorch’s built-in scheduler.

Uses torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts for periodic learning rate restarts. The restart interval and multiplier are configurable.

Returns:

PyTorch scheduler object.

Return type:

torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts

train_epoch() → float[source]¶

Execute one training epoch with gradient updates.

Iterates through the training DataLoader, computes loss, performs backpropagation, applies gradient clipping, and updates the model weights. Learning rate is updated at each step via the scheduler.

Returns:

Average training loss across all batches in the epoch.

Return type:

float

validate() → dict[source]¶

Evaluate the model on the validation dataset.

Computes forward passes without gradient computation and calculates various metrics including loss, accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.

Returns:

Dictionary containing validation metrics with keys:

  • ’loss’: Average validation loss (float)

  • ’accuracy’: Classification accuracy (float)

  • ’precision’: Macro-averaged precision (float)

  • ’recall’: Macro-averaged recall (float)

  • ’f1’: Macro-averaged F1-score (float)

Return type:

dict
